utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format specifies five items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, and total_budget. The final coverage plan includes all five categories. Each of the first three (health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage) is fully specified with plan details and explicit costs. The total_budget is also fully specified with monthly and annual breakdowns. The additional_services item is present and includes qualitative descriptions of included services, but it lacks a separate cost breakdown as required by the format ('any enhancement services with costs'). Therefore, four out of five items are fully specified according to the format.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes the following items: (1) Physical health plan: 'Value-Oriented Comprehensive Portfolio' — this name does not appear in the ground-truth ratings, so there is no direct rating match. (2) Mental health plan: 'InsurePlus Standard Plan' — found under primary_health_coverage → mental_health with a rating of 8. (3) Property coverage: 'InsurePlus Standard Coverage' — found under property_protection → home_content with a rating of 9. (4) Travel coverage: 'LifeFirst Standard Plan0' — this appears to be a typo or variant of 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' under mobility_coverage → travel_insurance, which has a rating of 9. Thus, we map these three identifiable items to their ratings, and note that 'Value-Oriented Comprehensive Portfolio' is unmatched in the ground-truth data.
